details.diagnosis: Task: infer the likely AIE mechanism (ICT vs TICT vs ESIPT vs neutral-aromatic/RIM vs unknown) for C28H14FN5S3. Working top1 is TICT; runner-up is neutral aromatic (RIM-dominated without a distinct twisted CT trap), with ICT also credible. Top1 is uncertain because the molecule is both fairly rigid/planar (high planarity proxy ~0.98, only ~4 rotatable bonds) and strongly push–pull (diarylamino donor and dicyano acceptor), so either (i) torsional CT quenching (TICT) or (ii) bright LE/ICT with AIE mainly from motion restriction could dominate. ESIPT is very unlikely due to lack of a proton donor. Shared prepared structure is available (neutral singlet, selected conformer 6, prepared XYZ/SDF ready). Macro can assess motif-level plausibility/external precedent and structural flexibility vs conjugation; Microscopic (bounded) can deliver one baseline S0 optimization + S1 vertical excitation to gauge excitation character proxies (e.g., oscillator strength, frontier orbital localization, dipole change) but cannot in round-1 map torsional pathways or find twisted minima. Both macro and microscopic evidence are needed now because structure-only cues are ambiguous between TICT and non-TICT (neutral aromatic/ICT), so we stay in portfolio screening rather than collapsing to a single pairwise contraction.
details.task_instruction: Run first-round Macro analysis and one bounded Microscopic baseline S0/S1 calculation on the shared prepared structure; return indicators relevant to distinguishing TICT vs ICT vs neutral-aromatic RIM (state brightness, CT character, and whether donor/acceptor orbitals are strongly separated).

agent_task_instructions.macro: Assess macro-level structural evidence relevant to the current working hypothesis 'TICT'. Reuse the shared prepared structure context when available, summarize low-cost structural indicators only, and stay within current macro capability. Hypothesis uncertainty to keep in mind: TICT is favored by the strong donor–acceptor design, but the molecule’s high predicted planarity/rigidity could keep it in a bright LE/ICT regime; a baseline S1 brightness/CT-character readout is needed to avoid over-calling TICT.
agent_task_instructions.microscopic: Run the first-round low-cost S0/S1 microscopic baseline task for the current working hypothesis 'TICT'. Reuse the shared prepared structure context when available. Prioritize semi-empirical or otherwise low-cost bounded evidence collection, not heavy exhaustive geometry optimization. Report local microscopic results only, and do not attempt mechanism discrimination beyond current microscopic capability. Capability note: Macro can screen mechanistic plausibility from substructure and flexibility but cannot quantify excited-state ordering/brightness. Microscopic (round-1) can run one low-cost baseline S0/S1 job on the provided conformer to estimate excitation energy and brightness and give coarse CT vs LE indicators; it cannot (in this round) locate twisted CT minima, conical intersections, or torsion-dependent nonradiative channels. Direct screening of TICT vs ICT via torsion sensitivity requires later dedicated scans/follow-ups.

hypothesis_pool.name: TICT
hypothesis_pool.rationale: Has a strong donor (N,N-diphenylamino) attached to an extended acceptor-rich core plus several rotatable bonds; classic setting where solution-state torsional relaxation forms a nonradiative CT minimum that aggregation suppresses.

hypothesis_pool.name: neutral aromatic
hypothesis_pool.rationale: Large, rigid, polyaromatic framework (high planarity proxy, multiple fused/linked rings) could AIE mainly via restriction of intramolecular motions without needing a distinct CT/twisted CT excited-state minimum.

hypothesis_pool.name: ICT
hypothesis_pool.rationale: Donor (diarylamino) to acceptor (dicyanovinyl-like) push–pull motif can yield emissive ICT states; needs screening against dark CT/TICT.

hypothesis_pool.name: unknown
hypothesis_pool.rationale: Could involve mixed mechanisms (e.g., local excitation + CT admixture, heavy-atom/S effects, state reordering) not decidable from structure alone.

hypothesis_pool.name: ESIPT
hypothesis_pool.rationale: No obvious labile proton donor (e.g., phenolic OH/NH) positioned for intramolecular H-bonded proton transfer; ESIPT unlikely.

hypothesis_screening_ledger.screening_note: Needs evidence whether S1 is primarily locally excited/bright with small charge separation and weak torsion-driving forces.
Needs evidence for a bright CT/ICT S1 without a twisted/dark CT minimum dominating relaxation.
Kept as residual: possible mixed LE/CT character or sulfur/heterocycle-driven state reordering.

Portfolio screening remains active because no alternative has been directly screened yet.

agent_task_instructions.Macro: From the provided canonical SMILES and structure summary (rotatable bonds=4, aromatic rings=5, planarity proxy~0.98, hetero atoms=9 including multiple S/N, donor diarylamino and acceptor dicyano motif), provide a mechanism-agnostic structural assessment: (1) identify donor/acceptor subunits and likely CT pathway; (2) qualitatively assess torsional freedom around donor–acceptor linkage(s) and whether twisted conformations are chemically plausible; (3) give a ranked plausibility among ICT/TICT/neutral-aromatic with 2–3 discriminative structural arguments and what experimental signatures would differentiate (solvatochromism, viscosity dependence, quantum yield trends). Do not make final decision—just evidence and screening suggestions.
agent_task_instructions.Microscopic: Perform exactly one baseline-only computation using the shared prepared structure (selected conformer id 6; use prepared XYZ). Route: aTB S0 geometry optimization (singlet, neutral) followed by a single S1 vertical excitation calculation (TD-style) with up to 5 excited states as configured. Report: S0 optimized geometry energy, key dihedral(s) around donor–core bond if available, S1 energy (eV/nm), oscillator strength f for lowest bright state(s), and any available qualitative orbital/transition characterization (donor→acceptor separation, CT vs LE indication such as hole/electron localization or dipole change if provided). No conformer scans, no torsion scans, no follow-ups.

Direct screening of TICT vs ICT via torsion sensitivity requires later dedicated scans/follow-ups.

hypothesis_reweight_explanation.ICT: Moderate prior due to clear push–pull motif, but could be superseded if baseline shows very low f and strong orbital separation suggestive of dark CT/TICT behavior.
hypothesis_reweight_explanation.TICT: Highest prior because donor–acceptor architecture plus available torsions commonly enable twisted dark CT relaxation that aggregation suppresses.
hypothesis_reweight_explanation.ESIPT: Downweighted to near-zero due to absence of an intramolecular H-bonded proton donor/acceptor pair needed for ESIPT.
hypothesis_reweight_explanation.neutral aromatic: Kept high because the scaffold is large and relatively rigid/planar, consistent with AIE dominated by restriction of residual motions and suppression of nonradiative decay without invoking a distinct TICT minimum.
hypothesis_reweight_explanation.unknown: Small residual to cover mixed LE/CT states or heteroatom/sulfur-driven state reordering not resolvable from initial evidence.

details.task_understanding: Collect and summarize macro/structural indicators that bear on the plausibility of a TICT (twisted intramolecular charge transfer) excited state for the given molecule, using only low-cost deterministic topology + geometry-proxy evidence and reusing the provided prepared-structure context.
details.reasoning_summary:
From the shared structure context and SMILES, the molecule is a strong donor–acceptor system (diarylamine donor appended to a heteroaromatic, cyano-substituted acceptor core), which is a classic prerequisite for TICT. However, the prepared-structure proxies indicate very high planarity/rigidity (planarity_proxy ~0.98) with low flexibility (rotatable bonds = 4; torsion_candidate_count = 4) and high compactness (~0.93). Such structural constraints tend to suppress large donor–acceptor twisting and instead favor a more planar LE/ICT state (brighter S1) unless there exists a specific low-barrier torsion at the donor–acceptor junction. Conformer_dispersion_proxy = 1.0 with 10 conformers suggests some accessible torsional variation, but without explicit dihedral readouts we can only flag “possible but structurally disfavored TICT” and identify the likely twist hinge (aryl–aryl/aryl–heteroaryl bond connecting the diarylamine to the acceptor core). Overall, macro evidence supports D–A CT capability while simultaneously warning that high intrinsic planarity may keep the system in a bright planar ICT regime rather than a fully twisted TICT minimum.
Capability limit note: This macro pass can only use the provided prepared-structure proxies and deterministic SMILES/topology reasoning; it cannot compute excited-state properties, dihedral energy barriers, or make a definitive TICT mechanism judgment. Any mention of TICT likelihood is therefore structural/qualitative and conditional.
details.execution_plan: Use deterministic_macro_structure_scan to execute the bounded macro workflow with focus areas: Identify likely donor (diarylamine) and acceptor (cyano + heteroaromatic/sulfur-containing core) substructures from canonical SMILES, Locate likely twist hinge(s): donor-to-core single bond(s) among the small set of rotatable bonds, Use planarity_proxy/compactness_proxy as structural constraints on TICT accessibility, Use conformer_count + dispersion_proxy as weak evidence for torsional accessibility. Planned local steps: [shared_context_load] Load the shared prepared structure context and reuse its descriptors. [focus_selection] Select the macro structural focus areas requested by the Planner instruction. [topology_analysis] Summarize rotor topology, ring systems, conjugation, and donor-acceptor layout. [geometry_proxy_analysis] Summarize planarity, torsion, compactness, and conformer-dispersion proxies.
details.result_summary:
The macro scan recorded aromatic_atom_count=30, hetero_atom_count=9, branch_point_count=13, rotatable_bond_count=4, planarity_proxy=0.979553, compactness_proxy=0.929255, and conformer_dispersion_proxy=1.0.
Key proxies: aromatic_atom_count=30, hetero_atom_count=9, branch_point_count=13, conjugation_proxy=8.98, flexibility_proxy=11.5, rotatable_bond_count=4, aromatic_ring_count=5, ring_system_count=4, donor_acceptor_partition_proxy=0.5, planarity_proxy=0.979553, compactness_proxy=0.929255, conformer_dispersion_proxy=1.0.
details.remaining_local_uncertainty: Macro evidence alone cannot resolve excited-state relaxation behavior or external consistency; unresolved local gap: Macro evidence is limited to single-molecule low-cost structural and geometry proxies, so it still cannot resolve excited-state relaxation behavior, aggregation-state packing, or external consistency.

details.task_understanding: Run a first-round, low-cost microscopic baseline (S0/S1) on the provided shared prepared structure for the working hypothesis "TICT". Use the shared prepared conformer when available and collect aTB-level S0 optimization plus a vertical excited-state manifold (vertical excitation energies and oscillator strengths) as the round-1 baseline deliverable. Do not attempt torsion scans, excited-state relaxation, or heavy full-DFT geometry optimization in this round.
details.reasoning_summary: The Planner-specified task_mode is baseline_s0_s1, and the registry exposes run_baseline_bundle for exactly this purpose. The run_baseline_bundle action supports a low-cost aTB S0 optimization plus vertical excited-state characterization and matches the requested deliverables. Reuse of the shared prepared structure is preferred by runtime policy; therefore launch a new low-cost baseline calculation on the provided prepared conformer with ground-state optimization enabled and request the default vertical-state window (up to 5 states) to capture the primary bright/nearby states for coarse CT/LE indication.
details.capability_limit_note: This baseline run is explicitly low-cost (aTB-level) and can provide an S0 optimized geometry, S0 properties (energy, dipole, Mulliken charges, HOMO-LUMO gap) and a vertical excited-state manifold (energies, oscillator strengths, state ordering). It cannot (in this round) locate twisted TICT minima, conical intersections, or perform torsion sensitivity scans or excited-state relaxation. It also will not perform heavy full-DFT geometry optimizations or validated high-level excited-state relaxations.
expected_outputs: Low-cost aTB S0 optimized geometry and final S0 energy/dipole/HOMO-LUMO gap and Mulliken charges.
Vertical excited-state manifold (vertical excitation energies and oscillator strengths) for the requested state window (first ~5 states).

Per-state ordering and brightness summary; coarse indicators of charge-transfer vs locally-excited character where simple aTB transition proxies (orbital contributions, dominant transitions) permit, but no guaranteed detailed CT-descriptor extraction.
Structured logs and provenance indicating use of the shared prepared conformer (prepared structure paths and selected_conformer_id).

details.reasoning_summary:
Local reasoning summary: The Planner-specified task_mode is baseline_s0_s1, and the registry exposes run_baseline_bundle for exactly this purpose. The run_baseline_bundle action supports a low-cost aTB S0 optimization plus vertical excited-state characterization and matches the requested deliverables. Reuse of the shared prepared structure is preferred by runtime policy; therefore launch a new low-cost baseline calculation on the provided prepared conformer with ground-state optimization enabled and request the default vertical-state window (up to 5 states) to capture the primary bright/nearby states for coarse CT/LE indication.
Capability limit note: This baseline run is explicitly low-cost (aTB-level) and can provide an S0 optimized geometry, S0 properties (energy, dipole, Mulliken charges, HOMO-LUMO gap) and a vertical excited-state manifold (energies, oscillator strengths, state ordering). It cannot (in this round) locate twisted TICT minima, conical intersections, or perform torsion sensitivity scans or excited-state relaxation. It also will not perform heavy full-DFT geometry optimizations or validated high-level excited-state relaxations.
Failure policy: If the baseline calculation fails or is unavailable, return a structured failure report including exit logs, the step at which it failed (geometry optimization or vertical excitation), and any partial artifacts produced. Do not attempt heavy/unsupported follow-ups automatically. The Planner should be informed to consider reattempt with adjusted budget or an alternative follow-up route (e.g., bounded conformer or torsion follow-up) if needed.
